key_insight: A truncated dual-variable augmented Lagrangian framework can turn stationarity of a smoothed nonconvex minimax reformulation into a finite-time KKT certificate for constrained problems with convex-composite objectives and functional inequalities.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a provable first-order method for hard constrained optimization models in PDEs and engineering design where feasibility, nonconvexity, and multiplier instability are major obstacles.
method: Restrict dual variables to a compact set and solve a smoothed prox-linear augmented Lagrangian minimax problem.
Use local conic regularity to show the truncation becomes inactive near feasible iterates and recover original KKT conditions.
Derive explicit KKT-residual rates: O(K^-1/3) with dual regularization, and O(K^-1/2) under stronger local structure without regularization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports nonasymptotic convergence guarantees, a finite-time feasibility mechanism, and explicit KKT residual rates under different regularity assumptions.
watch_item: The stronger O(K^-1/2) result depends on additional local structural assumptions and local error bounds, so performance may be less robust outside those regimes.
paper_title: Nonconvex Composite Functional Constraints via First-Order Augmented Lagrangian Methods under Local Regularity
